origin.name: Cyprus
origin.british_expat_population: Large British expat and retired community. Local funeral directors very experienced with UK repatriation.
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Cyprus is not widely available. Most repatriations are full body.
origin.source: FCDO Cyprus travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Nicosia guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom

airlines.name: easyJet
airlines.routes: LCA to LGW, MAN, BRS, EDI

special_rules: Death certificate is bilingual - no separate translation required for most purposes.
Large British expat community means local funeral directors are highly experienced.
Flights to UK are frequent and multiple routes are available from Larnaca.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Cypriot documentation must be in English (certificate is bilingual). The coroner is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Nicosia can register the death and advise on documentation. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Cyprus Repatriation to the UK: Every Step Explained
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Cyprus. Repatriation to the UK takes 7-14 days in a straightforward case. Documentation, flights and UK handover all handled for British families.

destinations.name: Ireland
special_rules: Irish Embassy in Nicosia handles consular matters.
Process identical to UK repatriation. Bilingual certificate accepted.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Cypriot documentation is accepted in English (bilingual certificate). The Coroner is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Nicosia can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Cyprus to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Cyprus and needs to come home to Ireland. Repatriation takes 7-14 days. All documentation and cargo flights handled. Contact us 24/7.
